extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu won stage 3 after ALAPHILIPPE Julian's decoy attack forced VAN AERT Wout to chase, delivering VAN DER POEL perfectly into the final 300 meters.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE Julian's leadout-disguised attack forced VAN AERT to burn matches chasing, sacrificing his own sprint for team tactics.
Deceuninck Quick-Step used BALLERINI Davide to stretch peloton before ALAPHILIPPE's feint, multi-phase softening tactic before finale.
Late-race crash with ~900m remaining reorganized sprint trains, allowing VAN DER POEL superior positioning while others scrambled.
VAN AERT admitted post-race he reacted to ALAPHILIPPE knowing it wasted energy, revealing tactical mistake under pressure.

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: converted tactical gift into win after frustrating previous day, showed maturity reading ALAPHILIPPE's decoy move
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: selfless false attack to burn VAN AERT's legs demonstrates team-first racing despite own GC position
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: admitted tactical error chasing ALAPHILIPPE unnecessarily, spent energy on opponent's wheel instead of positioning for sprint
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: positive: pre-finale stretch effort crucial to Deceuninck setup, showing domestique execution in high-speed finale
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: neutral: crashed in late-race pile-up alongside Bora-Hansgrohe riders, disrupting sprint preparation
